articles.title: Beijing uneasy as Trump–Xi summit preparations lose momentum - report
articles.snippet: The problem with being all show is that eventually you have to deliver real results. Earlier today there was a Politico report about Howard Lutnick and how the vast majority of his $18 trillion in touted investment deals into the US is basically smoke-and-mirrors, AI investment, or a repackaging of things that were already announced.Now, the SCMP is out with a report saying Chinese officials are unsettled about a planned April meeting between Xi and Trump because there could be nothing to announ

articles.title: China Two Sessions 2026: growth target, fiscal stance and 15th plan signals in focus
articles.snippet: China’s Two Sessions (4–11 March) is shaping up as a “targets + 15th plan signalling” event, more about direction and policy bias than a single mega-stimulus headline.Summary:China’s “Two Sessions” will open in Beijing on 4 March (CPPCC) and 5 March 2026 (NPC), with the headline policy statement coming via the Government Work Report by Premier Li Qiang on 5 March. 2026 is a key “bridge year” into the 15th five-year plan (2026–2030), with authorities preparing plan-related documents for March del

articles.title: China: Long-game growth strategy – Standard Chartered
articles.snippet: Standard Chartered economists Carol Liao and Shuang Ding expect China to set a 2026 GDP growth target of 4.5–5.0% at the National People’s Congress, slightly below 2025.

articles.title: The U.S. Just Took a Giant Step in The Rare Earth Race With China

combined_text:
[0.33] Beijing uneasy as Trump–Xi summit preparations lose momentum - report
The problem with being all show is that eventually you have to deliver real results. Earlier today there was a Politico report about Howard Lutnick and how the vast majority of his $18 trillion in touted investment deals into the US is basically smoke-and-mirrors, AI investment, or a repackaging of things that were already announced.Now, the SCMP is out with a report saying Chinese officials are unsettled about a planned April meeting between Xi and Trump because there could be nothing to announ

[0.33] China Two Sessions 2026: growth target, fiscal stance and 15th plan signals in focus
China’s Two Sessions (4–11 March) is shaping up as a “targets + 15th plan signalling” event, more about direction and policy bias than a single mega-stimulus headline.Summary:China’s “Two Sessions” will open in Beijing on 4 March (CPPCC) and 5 March 2026 (NPC), with the headline policy statement coming via the Government Work Report by Premier Li Qiang on 5 March. 2026 is a key “bridge year” into the 15th five-year plan (2026–2030), with authorities preparing plan-related documents for March del

[0.33] China: Long-game growth strategy – Standard Chartered
Standard Chartered economists Carol Liao and Shuang Ding expect China to set a 2026 GDP growth target of 4.5–5.0% at the National People’s Congress, slightly below 2025.
